---
title: "5.1.2 Uniform resource locators (URLs)"
subtitle: "The internet and the World Wide Web · P181"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Web Browsers

### Accessing web pages

<span class="term">Web browsers</span> are software that allow users to access and display web pages on their device screens.

Browsers interpret <span class="term">hypertext mark-up language (HTML)</span> (超文本标记语言) sent from websites and produce the results on the user's device.

## URLs

### Text addresses

<span class="term">Uniform resource locators (URLs)</span> (统一资源定位符) are text addresses used to access websites.

A URL is typed into a browser <span class="term">address bar</span> using the following format:

```text
protocol://website address/path/file name
```

## URL Components

### Protocol and website address

The <span class="term">protocol</span> is usually either <span class="term">http</span> or <span class="term">https</span>.

The website address is:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">domain host</span> (www)
- <span class="term">domain name</span> (website name)
- <span class="term">domain type</span> (.com, .org, .net, .gov, for example)
- sometimes <span class="term">country code</span> (.uk, .de, .cy, for example)
:::

## Path and File Name

### Location inside the website

The <span class="term">path</span> is the web page, but is often omitted and it then becomes the <span class="mark">root directory</span> of the website.

The <span class="term">file name</span> is the item on the web page.

Example:

```text
https://www.hoddereducation.co.uk/ict
```

## Classroom Check

### Parse a URL

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- URLs are <span class="mark">text addresses used to access websites</span>
- a URL is typed into the browser address bar
- the protocol is usually http or https
- the website address can include domain host, domain name, domain type and country code
- the path is the web page and the file name is the item on the web page
:::

## Bridge

### Next: HTTP and HTTPS

The next deck explains why `https` indicates a more secure way of sending and receiving data.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
